done_summary:
Completed triage of all 30 support tickets. Produced:

**`/workspace/triage_results.json`** — Structured JSON with:
- All 30 tickets classified into 12 categories with urgency levels
- 6 urgent tickets: 003 (login outage), 014/023/030 (API rate limits), 016 (API 500s), 028 (account compromise)
- 2 duplicate clusters: [007, 011] (dashboard permissions) and [014, 023, 030] (API rate limits)
- Category breakdown: 7 bug-report, 4 billing-refund, 4 spam-or-noise, 3 feature-request, 2 incident-active, 2 account-management, 2 auth-permissions, 2 general-question, 2 external-business, 1 security-incident, 1 legal-compliance

**`/workspace/README.md`** — Summary with recommended processing order and duplicate cluster explanations

**`/workspace/decisions/`** — 5 ADRs documenting non-obvious classification decisions:
- ADR-001: Ticket 014 rate limits → bug-report (not billing)
- ADR-002: Ticket 029 extortion → spam-or-noise (not security-incident)
- ADR-003: Ticket 016 API 500s → incident-active (not bug-report)
- ADR-004: Ticket 022 downtime refund → billing-refund
- ADR-005: Duplicate cluster detection methodology

All validations pass: correct JSON structure, valid categories/urgency, matching counts, sorted duplicate clusters, and proper duplicate_of references.
